task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code is well-structured, correctly handles edge cases, and meets all requirements.
trace: The function first checks if the input list is empty and returns an empty string if true. It then sorts the unique elements of the list in ascending order. Using a while loop, it iterates through the sorted list to identify consecutive runs of numbers. If a run is found (more than one number in sequence), it appends the range 'start-end' to the result list. If not, it appends the single number as a string. Finally, it joins all elements in the result list with commas and returns the resulting string. This approach ensures that consecutive runs are collapsed into ranges while handling edge cases like empty lists or lists with no consecutive numbers correctly.
